title: Cards
intro: Three card types cover every container need: Section for titled groups, MetricCard for single KPIs, and the multi-counter recipe for 2-4 related metrics. One shared shell — 1px border, 8px radius, token padding — replaces the ad-hoc Ant Cards, styled divs, and custom wrappers that used to coexist on every screen.

items.rule: Pick the card type by content purpose, not visual preference — Section for titled groups, MetricCard for one number, multi-counter for 2-4 facets of one whole.
items.why: When type follows purpose a dashboard explains itself; decorative card choices force readers to re-learn every screen.

items.rule: Build cards from the shared components — `Section` and `MetricCard` — never hand-rolled border+header+body divs.
items.why: ContractHeaderSidebar, ExampleDashboard, and SellerRFP each shipped near-identical card CSS before these components existed; hand-rolled copies drift apart immediately.

items.rule: Every card wears the same shell: `--bg-1` background, 1px `--border-default` border, 8px radius.
items.why: One shell makes cards read as one system — any deviation reads as a bug, not a choice.

items.rule: Cards are bordered, never shadowed — elevation is reserved for overlays (modals, popovers, drawers).
items.why: A shadow says the surface floats above the page; a card is part of the page.

items.rule: Card padding comes off the spacing scale — header `--space-3`/`--space-4` (12/16px), body `--space-4`/`--space-5` (16/24px) — never arbitrary values.
items.why: Token padding is what keeps side-by-side cards and their content aligned to the page grid.

items.rule: Stat cards in a row are structurally identical: same padding, same 12px/500 label, same value size and weight.
items.why: One inconsistent card makes the whole row read as four unrelated components.

items.rule: Apply `highlight` to at most one card per metric row.
items.why: Highlight marks the active filter or the page's primary metric; worn by every card it marks nothing.

items.rule: Give `bodyPadding='flush'` to any Section whose child owns its own padding — grids, tables, nested Sections.
items.why: Default body padding double-insets a GraviGrid and knocks its columns out of alignment with the page.

blocks.title: Card types
blocks.intro: Three types, chosen by what the content is — not by how you want the page to look.

rows.name: Section Card
rows.when: Content has a clear title and belongs to a named group — forms, detail panels, settings groups.

rows.name: Stat / Metric Card
rows.when: One key number needs to read at a glance — KPIs, counters, summary headers.

rows.name: Multi-Counter Card
rows.when: 2-4 related metrics that are facets of one whole — pipeline totals, status breakdowns. One shell, flex columns, 1px dividers.

blocks.title: Three card types
blocks.caption: Section Card, Stat/Metric Card, and Multi-Counter Card — each with its when-to-use. The choice is driven by content purpose.

blocks.title: Section Card — default
blocks.caption: Title on the left, muted subtitle next to it, action slot on the right. Header bar on --surface-muted with a bottom border; body padding 16/24px.

blocks.title: Section Card — compact density
blocks.caption: density='compact' + bodyPadding='compact' for sidebars and drawers: 8/12px header, 12/16px body that auto-stacks children with an 8px gap.

blocks.title: Section Card — flush body
blocks.caption: bodyPadding='flush' removes body padding entirely so a list, table, or GraviGrid runs edge-to-edge inside the card shell.

blocks.title: Section props
blocks.intro: Import from `@components/shared/Section`. Omit `title` to get a plain bordered container with no header bar.

rows.notes: Header bar title. Omit it and the component is just a bordered, rounded container — useful around a grid or chart that needs no redundant title.
Rendered before the title in --theme-color-1 (brand teal), matching the ActionFooter accent.
Short muted text next to the title — "3 fields", "updated 2m ago", a row count.
Right-side header slot: Edit/Expand buttons, filter toggles, or a StatusBadge describing section state.
Header padding 12/16px vs 8/12px. Compact for sidebars, drawers, and stacked dashboard sections.
standard = 16/24px. compact = 12/16px plus a flex column with 8px gap between children. flush = 0 — for grids, tables, nested Sections.
Body scrolls independently when it overflows. Use inside fixed-height flex columns.
Strips the outer border and radius. Use when the Section is embedded inside another bordered container and only the header bar is wanted.

blocks.title: MetricCard — default row
blocks.caption: valueFirst, left-aligned — the dashboard default. Tabular-numeric digits keep stacked values vertically aligned across the row.

blocks.title: MetricCard — sub and trend slots
blocks.caption: sub carries denominator context ("of 254 scheduled"); trend carries a quiet StatusBadge or delta chip. A metric card that tries to say four things says none of them.

blocks.title: MetricCard — highlight
blocks.caption: highlight adds a --theme-color-1 border to mark the active filter or the page's primary metric — exactly one per row.

blocks.title: MetricCard props
blocks.intro: Import from `@components/shared/MetricCard`. Value sizes: sm 20px, md 28px, lg 36px — label and sub stay constant so the number is the focal point.

rows.notes: Required. Keep to a phrase — "Active Deals", not "Number of active deals in the pipeline".
Required. The headline number; rendered with tabular-nums at 600 weight.
Supporting line below the value/label pair — denominators ("of 240"), recency ("last 30 days").
Slot for a quiet StatusBadge, delta chip, or sparkline. Keep it quiet.
Left scans better in rows. Center only when a single metric is the hero of a section.
valueFirst = big number on top (dashboard style). labelFirst = denser, better for many stats in a row.
sm for inline summary strips, md for page dashboards, lg when one metric headlines the view.
Brand-colored border marking the selected or primary metric. At most one per row.
Makes the card a real <button> with hover accent and focus-visible ring — use for drill-through.
Pass when the visible label alone is ambiguous, especially on clickable cards.

blocks.title: Multi-Counter Card
blocks.caption: One bordered shell, equal flex columns, 1px vertical dividers, no gap — the divider provides the separation.

blocks.title: Canonical skeleton
blocks.code:
import { useMemo } from 'react' import { Vertical, GraviButton, GraviGrid } from '@gravitate-js/excalibrr' import { Section } from '@components/shared/Section' import { MetricCard } from '@components/shared/MetricCard' import { StatusBadge } from '@components/shared/StatusBadge' const columnDefs = useMemo(() => getColumnDefs(), []) {/* Metric row — identical cards, one token gap */}
} />
{/* Section card — header bar + token-padded body */}
} > {/* fields */}
{/* Grid wrapper — flush body so the grid owns its own chrome */}
blocks.note: GraviButton takes buttonText, never children. GraviGrid always takes agPropOverrides (even empty) and memoized columnDefs. Money copy is decimal dollars — $0.0425/gal, never cents symbols.

blocks.title: Multi-counter recipe
blocks.code:
import { Fragment } from 'react' import { Texto } from '@gravitate-js/excalibrr'
{counters.map((c, i) => ( {i > 0 &&
}
{c.label} {c.value}
))}
blocks.note: No gap between columns — the 1px divider is the separation. overflow: hidden clips the dividers to the 8px radius. Cap at 4 counters; past that, switch to a MetricCard grid.

blocks.title: Card tokens
blocks.intro: The shell and spacing values every card type shares. No raw hex, no off-scale padding.

rows.use: Corner radius on every card type (literal — no token yet)
Section header vertical padding; compact body padding; gap between metric cards in a row
Section header horizontal padding; standard body vertical padding; MetricCard md vertical padding
Standard body horizontal padding; MetricCard md horizontal padding; stacking gap between cards on a page
highlight border, interactive hover accent, Section header icon
Trend deltas composed at the call site — never raw hex

items.do: Build cards from Section and MetricCard with token-based spacing.
items.dont: Hand-roll a card div with an arbitrary shadow, hard-coded font sizes, and off-scale padding.
items.why: Hand-rolled cards break theming and drift from the system the moment they're copied.

items.do: Keep every stat card in a row identical — 16/24px padding, 12px/500 label, one value size.
items.dont: Tweak padding, border weight, or value weight per card.
items.why: The row only reads as one summary when the cards are interchangeable.

items.do: Highlight exactly one card — the active filter or the headline metric.
items.dont: Spread highlight across the row for decoration.
items.why: Highlight is a pointer, not a paint job.

items.do: Write money values in decimal dollars — $0.0425/gal.
items.dont: Use cents symbols or mixed units (4.25¢/gal).
items.why: Gravitate copy standard: decimal dollars everywhere — values, axes, inputs.

items.title: Muted card labels use appearance='medium', not 'secondary'
items.detail: Texto appearance='secondary' is BLUE in Excalibrr, not gray. For the muted gray of card labels and sublines, use appearance='medium'.

items.title: Clickable MetricCard is a real <button>
items.detail: Passing onClick renders the card as a button element. Never put another interactive control (button, link) inside its trend slot — nested interactive elements are invalid HTML and break keyboard navigation. Pair drill-through cards with ariaLabel.

items.title: Accent comes from --theme-color-1 only
items.detail: highlight, hover, and header icons all use --theme-color-1. Do not reach for --theme-color-2 — it swings green↔blue across themes and will desync the card accent from the rest of the page.

items.title: There is no built-in negative style
items.detail: MetricCard ships no 'danger' variant. Compose negative or alert states at the call site with --status-danger-text or a quiet StatusBadge in the trend slot, so the severity decision stays with the page.

items.title: Body horizontal padding is 24px, not 20px
items.detail: The demo caption visible in the Section default specimen reads "Body default padding is 16/20px", and the Section.tsx doc comment repeats it. Both are stale. The shipped CSS is padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-5), and tokens.css sets --space-5 to 24px — so the rendered body is 16/24px. Trust the CSS, not the demo caption.

blocks.title: Composing cards on a page
blocks.body:
Lay metric rows out with CSS grid — `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(160px, 1fr))` and a `--space-3` gap — so cards stay equal-width and wrap cleanly at narrow widths. Stack cards and sections down the page with `--space-5` (24px).

Reach for the multi-counter when the numbers are facets of one whole (a pipeline's quoted/awarded/pending/rejected); reach for a MetricCard grid when each number stands alone or needs sub, trend, or drill-through. The multi-counter is deliberately minimal — no per-column highlight or click behavior.

Omit the Section title to wrap a chart or embedded widget in a plain bordered container without a redundant title bar. When Sections nest, only the outermost carries the border — give inner ones `flush` so borders never stack.
